sub: Your two deliverables: present a topic in depth, and survive the airspace

title: Presentation Topics 1–6: Systems & Autonomy
desc: Pick one topic — first come, first served

rows: Real-Time and Embedded Specification for Java (RTSJ 2.0)
How the Java platform supports real-time and embedded systems through improved memory management and deterministic execution
AI and Machine Learning in Drone Control Systems
How AI/ML increase drone autonomy and efficiency in complex environments
Drones and 5G: The Future of Aviation Communication
The role of 5G in communication and real-time data transfer between drones and control centers
Air Traffic Management for UAVs
Concepts for integrating drones into existing air traffic and managing drone flights in urban airspace
Energy Efficiency and Battery Technologies
Advances in battery technology and alternative energy sources for longer flight times
Collision Avoidance in Dense Airspace
LIDAR, ultrasound and camera systems for safe navigation — see the BlueSky plugins/asas module (also the competition topic!)
Flight Control Systems for Autonomous Drones
Software and algorithms required to control autonomous drones, and their impact on safety and efficiency
Air Traffic Modeling and Simulation for Drones
Models to predict and simulate drone traffic in different scenarios and its influence on traditional air traffic
Drones in the Smart Cities of the Future
Delivery of goods, infrastructure inspection and environmental protection in smart cities
Blockchain-Based Drone Data Management
Using blockchain to guarantee data integrity and security in drone networks
Security and Privacy in Drone Air Traffic
Security challenges of operating drones in public and private airspace
Regulation and Legal Frameworks for Drones
Existing legal rules and their evolution towards safe drone operation
Multi-Drone Operations and Coordination
Technology and algorithms for coordinating multiple drones in shared airspace
Drones in Commercial Air Transport: The Future of Logistics
The potential of drones for commercial cargo and the impact on the logistics industry
Optimizing Drone Deployment in Disaster Areas
Traffic models for efficient drone use in emergencies — fast relief and crisis logistics
Airport Infrastructure and Drones
Infrastructure changes and technologies to integrate UAVs into existing airport operations, e.g. inspection and maintenance

title: The Competition: Collision Avoidance in BlueSky
desc: We put theory aside and let your code fight for survival

items: We split into groups — each group develops a collision-avoidance system for the airspace
All systems run in the BlueSky air traffic simulator (TU Delft, open source)
Scoring is brutally simple: the group that survives longest without a collision wins
Starting point: the bluesky/plugins/asas conflict-resolution plugins — study them, then beat them
Setup, scenarios and rules of the game: Module 12

title: Expectations: The Path to Grade 1.0
desc: Two hard expectations — and what achieving them looks like

rows: ≥ 60 hours of independent homework across the semester
The effort is visible over time — iterations, notes, commits — not a last-week sprint
You are able to explain your practical project
In the Q&A you can justify every design decision and answer “what happens if we change X?” — “the tutorial/AI did it that way” does not count
Depth on your seminar topic
You go beyond the first search hit: working examples plus your own critical assessment
Honest engineering in the competition
You can explain detection horizon, resolution maneuver and priorities — not just parameters tweaked until it worked

items: Grade 1.0 = all four convincingly. Understanding is the currency of this course: if you built it, you can explain it — and modify it on request
Start early — the simulator, the standards, the SDK and the data formats all have a learning curve
